affected_locations.detail: Raw bounded natural language is accepted as an unverified allergen.

affected_locations.label: system_context_construction
affected_locations.detail: The raw string enters an AllergyCheck message.

affected_locations.label: system_context_serialization
affected_locations.detail: The message is serialized into DRUG_CONTEXT.

affected_locations.label: privilege_boundary_sink
affected_locations.detail: DRUG_CONTEXT is installed after the base prompt as an additional system-role message.

affected_locations.label: incomplete_output_control
affected_locations.detail: Output validation scans markup and structured identities, not medical meaning in summary or urgency copy.

fix_direction: Use canonical structured display labels for server-authored safety copy, never interpolate raw client prose into DRUG_CONTEXT, and reject medical-instruction or reassurance tokens at this boundary.
review_tier: P0 blocker: validated AGENTS section 2 invariant bypass
rationale: Raw request prose is deterministically stamped into the server safety panel and a privileged system message. A label can include reassurance such as safe while still matching after parenthesis stripping, directly bypassing the AGENTS §2-2 server-authored allergy boundary; this is P0.

source: A source-qualified checkout such as git checkout HEAD -- .githooks/pre-commit or git checkout main -- a modified task file.
closest_control: The presence of any source ref is treated as proof the checkout is safe, although it still replaces the named worktree and index content.
sink: Git overwrites local modifications in an attacker-selected tracked file.
impact: An agent can discard unrelated work or revert a modified safety/secret control without triggering the destructive-checkout denial.
preconditions: A delegated agent executes a source-qualified checkout against a modified tracked file while the repository hook is the enforcement boundary.
counterevidence: The impact is local repository integrity, requires a modified tracked file and delegated Git action, and does not expose the deployed web application directly.

evidence.claim: The policy denies checkout -- only when no non-option source ref precedes the separator.
The suite affirmatively requires git checkout main -- README.md to remain allowed.

affected_locations.label: root_control
affected_locations.detail: The policy denies checkout -- only when no non-option source ref precedes the separator.

affected_locations.label: negative_control
affected_locations.detail: The suite affirmatively requires git checkout main -- README.md to remain allowed.

fix_direction: Block source-qualified checkout overwrites unless the exact paths and destructive effect are explicitly approved and within task scope.
review_tier: P2: validated local developer-integrity gap below the repository's P0/P1 care-impact budget
rationale: Source-qualified checkout deterministically overwrites tracked work, but the demonstrated effect is privileged local developer-work loss. A discarded file could contain a safety or secret control, but no deployed care or secret-disclosure path is established; the equivalent checkout sink in R01-CAN-003 is P2.

source: Government retrieval result containing two distinct itemSeq records with the same Korean display name but different source IDs, ingredients, or narratives.
closest_control: Source and ingredient mismatches normally fail closed, and product counts are bounded; the prompt and validation maps nevertheless use different cardinalities under a name collision.
sink: One record silently disappears from server grounding while both remain model-visible, creating ambiguous identity for card validation and server stamping.
impact: The model can summarize one same-name record while the server attributes and validates the card against another, potentially presenting the wrong indication or caution under a valid source.
preconditions: Two retrieved government products share nameKo but differ in itemSeq, source, ingredients, or narrative, and the model emits a card resolved through that ambiguous name.
counterevidence: No checked-in fixture has a same-name collision, most source or ingredient mismatches fail closed, and retrieval deduplicates by itemSeq with bounded product counts.
proof_gaps: A real or synthetic same-name collision must demonstrate that mixed semantics survive validation and server stamping.

evidence.claim: GroundedDrug records are keyed solely by drug.nameKo(), so a later same-name record overwrites an earlier one.
Every retrieved record, including same-name records, is still rendered to the model.
Validation resolves the model card through the collapsed name-keyed map.
Retrieval deduplicates by itemSeq, not display name, so same-name distinct records are not inherently removed.

affected_locations.label: root_control
affected_locations.detail: GroundedDrug records are keyed solely by drug.nameKo(), so a later same-name record overwrites an earlier one.

affected_locations.label: divergent_prompt
affected_locations.detail: Every retrieved record, including same-name records, is still rendered to the model.

affected_locations.label: identity_consumer
affected_locations.detail: Validation resolves the model card through the collapsed name-keyed map.

affected_locations.label: retrieval_identity_control
affected_locations.detail: Retrieval deduplicates by itemSeq, not display name, so same-name distinct records are not inherently removed.

fix_direction: Ground and address products by immutable itemSeq or sourceRefId rather than display name, and require the model card to carry that server-issued identity.
review_tier: conditional P0: required clinical, provider, deployment, or runtime proof is unresolved
rationale: The prompt keeps both records while the grounding map overwrites one by name, so the structural identity mismatch is real. P0 provenance and medical-fact impact remains conditional on a collision that survives the remaining checks.

source: Anonymous user's newest consultation message sent verbatim to the configured provider for term extraction.
closest_control: The application avoids logging the text and sets store=false on pass 2, but pass 1 relies on the provider's unspecified default.
sink: Sensitive symptom text may be eligible for provider-side storage even though the sibling completion path explicitly opts out.
impact: Consultation content can persist at the third-party LLM provider contrary to the intended ephemeral handling, depending on provider semantics.
preconditions: The configured compatible provider interprets an omitted store field as permitting persistence and extraction is invoked with consultation text.
counterevidence: The application does not log the extraction text, pass 2 explicitly sends store=false, and a provider may default to no storage or retain abuse logs regardless of this field.
proof_gaps: The configured provider's default storage and retention contract is not present in target objects.

evidence.claim: The extraction request sends raw newest-user text but does not set store=false.
The full-answer request explicitly sets store=false.
The text sent can contain symptoms and medicine questions.

affected_locations.label: root_control
affected_locations.detail: The extraction request sends raw newest-user text but does not set store=false.

affected_locations.label: contrasting_privacy_control
affected_locations.detail: The full-answer request explicitly sets store=false.

affected_locations.label: sensitive_source
affected_locations.detail: The text sent can contain symptoms and medicine questions.

fix_direction: Send store=false explicitly on extraction requests and document and verify the configured provider's retention and abuse-monitoring behavior.
review_tier: conditional P0: required clinical, provider, deployment, or runtime proof is unresolved
rationale: Pass 1 sends symptom text without the sibling no-storage flag, but omission does not itself prove third-party persistence. If provider semantics permit storage this bypasses §2-5 and is P0; provider verification is required.

source: One anonymous extraction request accepted within the prior input and aggregate-admission controls reaches a provider call without an explicit output-token ceiling.
closest_control: A strict schema, 60-second timeout, and 4 MiB decoder cap bound neighboring effects, but completeJson sets no explicit pass-1 max_tokens value.
sink: Provider output generation for one admitted extraction call occurs before parser term and string-length caps apply.
impact: If configured-provider defaults permit materially excessive schema-constrained output, one admitted call can consume additional quota and worker time.
preconditions: The configured provider permits materially larger pass-1 output without max_tokens than the intended extraction result, and the extra generation produces P1-level quota or worker impact.
counterevidence: The strict schema caps array cardinality, the response decoder is capped at 4 MiB, the call times out after 60 seconds, and provider defaults may impose a small output limit.
Oversized input and aggregate concurrency or spend are already reported by R01-CAN-022 and R01-CAN-023 and are not new impact here.
proof_gaps: A bounded counting and delaying fake-provider harness has not established materially excessive pass-1 output or P1 resource consumption for one admitted request under the configured provider semantics.

evidence.claim: completeJson sets model, stream, schema, and messages, but no input cap, max_tokens, n, or provider work budget beyond a timeout.
Every nonblank newest message blocks the MVC request on extraction before parser caps run.
The decoded response is bounded at 4 MiB, limiting memory but not provider token generation.
The default extraction budget is 60 seconds.

affected_locations.label: root_control
affected_locations.detail: completeJson sets model, stream, schema, and messages, but no input cap, max_tokens, n, or provider work budget beyond a timeout.

affected_locations.label: blocking_caller
affected_locations.detail: Every nonblank newest message blocks the MVC request on extraction before parser caps run.

affected_locations.label: response_memory_control
affected_locations.detail: The decoded response is bounded at 4 MiB, limiting memory but not provider token generation.

affected_locations.label: timeout_control
affected_locations.detail: The default extraction budget is 60 seconds.

fix_direction: After the residual is reproduced, set a small explicit pass-1 output-token ceiling; retain the separate input and aggregate-admission remediations under R01-CAN-022 and R01-CAN-023.
review_tier: conditional P1: provider output amplification remains unvalidated
rationale: The missing pass-1 max_tokens setting is real, but oversized input and aggregate admission are already retained findings. The only new residual depends on provider output behavior that target objects do not establish, so it remains needs_review at conditional P1.

source: Configured OpenAI-compatible provider or model that answers 400 to strict response_format, including a provider behavior change.
closest_control: Extraction fails safely to no medicines and logs a fixed error reason; unlike pass 2, it neither checks supportsStructuredOutput nor performs a bounded schema fallback.
sink: An otherwise usable configured model can never produce search terms, so every medicine question loses the verified retrieval feature.
impact: Provider compatibility drift causes persistent availability loss of the core verified-medicine path, leaving users only an unavailable/no-medicine response.
preconditions: An operator configures an OpenAI-compatible provider or model that rejects strict response_format.
counterevidence: The failure is safe, yields no medicine context, the default model is documented as compatible, and no untrusted caller controls the model configuration.

evidence.claim: completeJson always includes strict response_format and has no unsupported-schema retry.
Pass 2 consults the structured-output allowlist and can operate prompt-only.
Schema support is explicitly variable and governed by an allowlist.
Extraction failure becomes an empty query, disabling verified retrieval for the turn.

affected_locations.label: root_control
affected_locations.detail: completeJson always includes strict response_format and has no unsupported-schema retry.

affected_locations.label: contrasting_control
affected_locations.detail: Pass 2 consults the structured-output allowlist and can operate prompt-only.

affected_locations.label: configuration_contract
affected_locations.detail: Schema support is explicitly variable and governed by an allowlist.

affected_locations.label: availability_sink
affected_locations.detail: Extraction failure becomes an empty query, disabling verified retrieval for the turn.

fix_direction: Treat this as reliability work: reuse the structured-output compatibility allowlist and bounded fallback used by pass 2.
review_tier: not reportable under the AGENTS security-review boundary
rationale: The source is trusted deployment configuration and the path fails closed to an empty verified-medicine context. It is a real compatibility defect but not a security-boundary violation, so it is not reportable.

source: User message containing an instruction designed to make pass 1 return an unrelated, prescription, or otherwise inappropriate valid INN.
closest_control: The extraction prompt says never follow instructions and the parser caps three INN-shaped terms; no server-side semantic relation binds a term to the stated symptoms.
sink: A manipulated but real ingredient can be officially retrieved, giving later cards valid government provenance even though selection was inappropriate.
impact: The user can receive an apparently verified medicine result chosen by prompt injection rather than by the intended symptom-to-search task.
preconditions: The configured provider follows a prompt-injection payload, returns an inappropriate but valid INN, government data returns a product, and the final answer renders it as relevant care information.
counterevidence: The extraction prompt forbids instructions, term count and syntax are bounded, allergy declarations suppress proposals, card facts remain grounded, and the caller influences only its own stateless response.
proof_gaps: A controlled configured-provider and government-fixture reproduction plus qualified clinical review must establish an inappropriate rendered selection.

evidence.claim: User text is sent to a model that chooses search ingredients; prompt wording is the appropriateness control.
Parsing validates shape and INN-like syntax, not medical appropriateness or origin.
The extraction model receives the raw newest user message.
Accepted ingredients are retrieved from government sources and become grounded context.
Retrieval ranks OTC first but does not make the model-selected ingredient clinically appropriate.

affected_locations.label: model_boundary
affected_locations.detail: User text is sent to a model that chooses search ingredients; prompt wording is the appropriateness control.

affected_locations.label: syntactic_only_control
affected_locations.detail: Parsing validates shape and INN-like syntax, not medical appropriateness or origin.

affected_locations.label: provider_call
affected_locations.detail: The extraction model receives the raw newest user message.

affected_locations.label: grounding_sink
affected_locations.detail: Accepted ingredients are retrieved from government sources and become grounded context.

affected_locations.label: retrieval_control
affected_locations.detail: Retrieval ranks OTC first but does not make the model-selected ingredient clinically appropriate.

fix_direction: Add a clinically reviewed server-side selection constraint or clearly separate model-selected query provenance from any implication of symptom appropriateness.
review_tier: conditional P0: required clinical, provider, deployment, or runtime proof is unresolved
rationale: The parser proves only INN shape and government retrieval proves only existence, not appropriateness. Official-provenance laundering would be P0 safety impact, but model obedience and clinical inappropriateness are not yet validated.

source: A contributor-controlled or operator-chosen secret value that matches mermaid.<letters>.v<digits>, placed in a credential-like assignment in any staged file.
closest_control: The allowlist is value-scoped rather than path-scoped, but the value language is broader than the finite browser-storage constants it intends to exempt.
sink: A default gitleaks finding whose extracted secret matches the regex can be globally suppressed before a public commit.
impact: A matching real credential could enter public Git history without the repository's secret scan stopping it.
preconditions: A real secret value matches mermaid.<letters>.v<digits>, a gitleaks detector would otherwise flag it, and a staged file is scanned with the target allowlist.
counterevidence: Common provider-issued keys do not use this format, the regex is intended for browser-storage constants, and no real secret suppression was demonstrated.
proof_gaps: A target-version gitleaks reproduction must show an otherwise-detected credential being suppressed by this value allowlist.

evidence.claim: A repository-wide secret-value allowlist accepts mermaid.<letters>.v<digits> with unbounded letter and digit lengths and no finite storage-key name set.

affected_locations.label: root_control
affected_locations.detail: A repository-wide secret-value allowlist accepts mermaid.<letters>.v<digits> with unbounded letter and digit lengths and no finite storage-key name set.

fix_direction: Scope allowlisting to exact finite storage-key values or the precise rule and path, and add a red test using a detector-triggering credential-shaped value.
review_tier: conditional P0: required clinical, provider, deployment, or runtime proof is unresolved
rationale: The regex is globally value-scoped and broader than its stated constants, but regex overlap alone does not prove a real detector bypass. A demonstrated secret bypass would violate §2-8 and be P0, so the candidate needs review.

source: An external provider response contains a slash-dense ingredient field near the 4MB body limit.
closest_control: The body cap and MAX_ROWS constrain input bytes, but no per-field length or maximum ingredient count limits post-parse allocation.
sink: String.split and distinct materialize a potentially very large array/stream/list.
impact: A malformed remote response may cause request CPU/heap exhaustion and then be retried by public callers.
preconditions: The fixed provider returns a near-4 MiB slash-dense ingredient field, a public request triggers parsing, and available heap makes the split expansion material.
counterevidence: The 4 MiB decoder limit is a hard byte bound, MAX_ROWS limits row count, the provider is a fixed trusted fact source, and impact has not been benchmarked.
proof_gaps: A target-JVM benchmark or crafted provider response must establish material CPU or heap amplification.

evidence.claim: External ITEM_INGR_NAME and MAIN_INGR_ENG flow into splitIngredients.
raw.split('/') allocates all segments, trims and distincts with no field/segment bound.
The full decoded response is limited to 4MB, but expansion after decoding is not separately bounded.
The full decoded response is limited to 4MB, but expansion after decoding is not separately bounded.

affected_locations.label: source
affected_locations.detail: External ITEM_INGR_NAME and MAIN_INGR_ENG flow into splitIngredients.

affected_locations.label: root_control
affected_locations.detail: raw.split('/') allocates all segments, trims and distincts with no field/segment bound.

affected_locations.label: closest_control_1
affected_locations.detail: The full decoded response is limited to 4MB, but expansion after decoding is not separately bounded.

affected_locations.label: closest_control_2
affected_locations.detail: The full decoded response is limited to 4MB, but expansion after decoding is not separately bounded.

fix_direction: Reject oversized ingredient fields and cap segment count before split or list materialization.
review_tier: conditional P2: required clinical, provider, deployment, or runtime proof is unresolved
rationale: Unbounded split and distinct allocation exists inside a bounded response, but the provider-control precondition and actual amplification are unresolved. It remains a conditional P2 resource candidate.

source: Provider-authored malformed JSON or HTTP error details generated while answering sensitive user symptoms, potentially containing response fragments.
closest_control: The public response is a safe server fallback, but the internal pass-2 catch does not apply the fixed-vocabulary logging discipline used by extraction.
sink: A decoder or provider exception that embeds response text can persist model-generated health content in application logs.
impact: Sensitive consultation-derived prose may enter durable logs and become visible to operators or log readers.
preconditions: A provider or decoder exception message or cause quotes model-generated consultation content and the pass-2 catch logs the full Throwable.
counterevidence: The client receives a generic fallback, ordinary timeout or status exceptions may contain no response body, and extraction uses fixed-vocabulary logging.
proof_gaps: An exact target Spring/provider canary reproduction must show consultation-derived response text in the logged Throwable.

evidence.claim: HTTP, timeout, and JSON decode exceptions from a model response propagate to the controller.
The catch logs the complete throwable and stack trace at ERROR.
The extraction path deliberately logs only exception type and a fixed reason because parser errors can quote consultation content.

affected_locations.label: error_source
affected_locations.detail: HTTP, timeout, and JSON decode exceptions from a model response propagate to the controller.

affected_locations.label: log_sink
affected_locations.detail: The catch logs the complete throwable and stack trace at ERROR.

affected_locations.label: negative_control
affected_locations.detail: The extraction path deliberately logs only exception type and a fixed reason because parser errors can quote consultation content.

fix_direction: Log only fixed, value-free error metadata for pass 2 and exclude Throwable messages, response bodies, and causes from persistent logs.
review_tier: conditional P0: required clinical, provider, deployment, or runtime proof is unresolved
rationale: The full-Throwable sink is present, but the claimed sensitive source reaches it only if the exact exception embeds response content. If reproduced this is a §2-5 P0; current target objects leave that framework/provider fact unresolved.

source: A compromised/malformed government facility record supplies markup-like nameKo content; ordinary users do not control this field directly.
closest_control: escapeHtml protects the explicit icon HTML string, and React safely renders the list/detail copy; the raw SDK title option is left to third-party implementation behavior.
sink: Naver Marker receives raw untrusted title text; if the SDK interpolates it into HTML rather than using a safe DOM property, scriptable markup can execute in the app origin.
impact: A malicious facility name could execute same-origin JavaScript and steal consultation/allergy/location storage or falsify medical-access UI.
preconditions: A compromised or malformed provider name contains markup, the pinned Naver SDK materializes Marker.title as unsafe HTML, and a user loads the affected map.
counterevidence: React text and explicit marker HTML escape the facility name, marker title APIs normally use text or attribute semantics, no SDK implementation is in target objects, and ordinary users do not control provider names.
proof_gaps: A browser probe against the exact deployed Naver SDK must show Marker.title becoming executable HTML.

evidence.claim: facility.nameKo from the server/public-data boundary is passed raw as Naver Marker.title.
The same name is explicitly escaped before inclusion in marker icon HTML, showing the code recognizes the HTML-string sink.
The hostile-name regression test inspects icon.content only and does not observe how the real SDK materializes Marker.title.

affected_locations.label: untrusted_source
affected_locations.detail: facility.nameKo from the server/public-data boundary is passed raw as Naver Marker.title.

affected_locations.label: known_safe_parallel_path
affected_locations.detail: The same name is explicitly escaped before inclusion in marker icon HTML, showing the code recognizes the HTML-string sink.

affected_locations.label: partial_test_control
affected_locations.detail: The hostile-name regression test inspects icon.content only and does not observe how the real SDK materializes Marker.title.

fix_direction: Remove the redundant raw title or pass an explicitly escaped or safe text value, and test the real SDK with a hostile provider name.
review_tier: conditional P1: required clinical, provider, deployment, or runtime proof is unresolved
rationale: Only a conditional third-party title sink remains after first-party escaping. Without actual SDK HTML behavior, the XSS chain is not centrally validated; P1 is conditional on that proof.

source: The victim's exact device/manual coordinate is sensitive input; infrastructure operators or log readers are the relevant unauthorized observers.
closest_control: The request is same-origin and normally protected by HTTPS, the browser fetch does not add a history entry, and no target backend source explicitly logs coordinates.
sink: Full-precision coordinates are embedded in the request target, which reverse proxies, CDNs, access logs, and observability systems commonly record.
impact: Infrastructure retention can turn an ephemeral nearby-care search into a durable, linkable location record.
preconditions: Deployment infrastructure retains full request targets, users submit precise coordinates, and retained logs are accessible beyond the immediate request processor.
counterevidence: HTTPS protects passive transit, fetch creates no browser-history entry, and target backend source contains no explicit coordinate access log.
proof_gaps: Production reverse-proxy, CDN, access-log, observability, retention, and reader-access configuration are not in target objects.

evidence.claim: Exact device or manual latitude/longitude become each facility query.
Exact device or manual latitude/longitude become each facility query.
The full-precision values are encoded as lat and lng query parameters on a GET URL.

affected_locations.label: source_1
affected_locations.detail: Exact device or manual latitude/longitude become each facility query.

affected_locations.label: source_2
affected_locations.detail: Exact device or manual latitude/longitude become each facility query.

affected_locations.label: url_sink
affected_locations.detail: The full-precision values are encoded as lat and lng query parameters on a GET URL.

fix_direction: Avoid full-precision location in GET targets where practical, redact or quantize coordinate logs, and define retention and access controls.
review_tier: conditional P2: required clinical, provider, deployment, or runtime proof is unresolved
rationale: Precise coordinates deterministically enter the URL, but durable infrastructure retention is a deployment fact absent from the target. The privacy finding therefore needs review.

source: A matched breathing emergency involving asthma, anaphylaxis, or another condition for which the person has prescribed rescue medication.
closest_control: The code correctly prioritizes 119 and avoids model-authored wording, but has no reason-specific or clinically approved exception to the blanket medication instruction.
sink: The server tells every emergency user, 'Do not ... take any medicine first.'
impact: A user may interpret the trusted banner as an instruction to delay epinephrine or a rescue inhaler while seeking emergency help.
preconditions: A matched emergency involves a condition for which prescribed rescue medication is appropriate and the user follows the blanket no-medicine instruction.
counterevidence: The response correctly prioritizes calling 119, is server-authored rather than model-authored, and discouraging unsupervised self-medication may be intentional.
proof_gaps: Qualified clinical review must approve reason-specific wording and any rescue-medication exception.

evidence.claim: Every reason code receives the blanket instruction not to take any medicine first.
The emergency message is rendered as the authoritative error banner.

affected_locations.label: root_control_and_sink
affected_locations.detail: Every reason code receives the blanket instruction not to take any medicine first.

affected_locations.label: rendered_sink
affected_locations.detail: The emergency message is rendered as the authoritative error banner.

fix_direction: Replace the blanket medication sentence with clinically approved reason-specific copy while preserving immediate 119 escalation and avoiding model authority.
review_tier: conditional P0: required clinical, provider, deployment, or runtime proof is unresolved
rationale: The blanket instruction is deterministic and authoritative, but whether it delays epinephrine or a rescue inhaler is a clinical judgment. If confirmed, unsafe emergency copy is a P0 §2 safety defect; central review is required.

source: A valid model answer classifying a non-rule-matched condition as urgent and placing care timing in urgency.title/message.
closest_control: The backend preserves a typed urgent state, but both first-party render paths gate the urgency copy on the emergency enum value only.
sink: Urgent title/message never reaches the screen unless the model duplicates it in summary, which no invariant requires.
impact: A user may miss a recommendation to seek prompt care because the structured urgency information is silently discarded.
preconditions: A schema-valid answer uses urgency.level=urgent and places care timing in urgency.title or urgency.message without duplicating it in summary.
counterevidence: The model may duplicate timing in summary, but neither the schema nor validator requires it, and the emergency rendering branch works correctly.

evidence.claim: urgent is a valid urgency level with title and message fields.
The model urgency object is preserved in the validated response.
Only level === 'emergency' renders urgency.title and urgency.message.
The cross-tab safety alert also keys solely on emergencyActive.
The cross-tab safety alert also keys solely on emergencyActive.
The banner is rendered only when urgency.level is exactly emergency; urgent and unknown title/message fields are silently skipped.
AnsweredTurn is the production rendering sink and has no alternate non-emergency urgency rendering branch.

affected_locations.label: entrypoint/wrapper
affected_locations.detail: urgent is a valid urgency level with title and message fields.

affected_locations.label: entrypoint/wrapper
affected_locations.detail: The model urgency object is preserved in the validated response.

affected_locations.label: root_control
affected_locations.detail: Only level === 'emergency' renders urgency.title and urgency.message.

affected_locations.label: sink_1
affected_locations.detail: The cross-tab safety alert also keys solely on emergencyActive.

affected_locations.label: sink_2
affected_locations.detail: The cross-tab safety alert also keys solely on emergencyActive.

affected_locations.label: root_control
affected_locations.detail: The banner is rendered only when urgency.level is exactly emergency; urgent and unknown title/message fields are silently skipped.

affected_locations.label: sink
affected_locations.detail: AnsweredTurn is the production rendering sink and has no alternate non-emergency urgency rendering branch.

fix_direction: Render urgent title and message in an accessible first-party banner or status surface on chat and cross-tab views.
review_tier: P1 blocker: validated care, safety, privacy, availability, or control impact under the AGENTS review lens
rationale: Urgent is a valid preserved backend state, yet both first-party render paths gate the safety copy on emergency only. This deterministically hides care-timing information and meets the repository's P1 safety-information lens.

source: The values originate in external government API responses; an anonymous caller can trigger their processing but does not select a facility's schedule values.
closest_control: The parser intentionally treats zero as closed and tolerates invalid values, but the adapters separately document that a closed day is represented by absent fields and preserve any present zero endpoints.
sink: A legitimate 00:00 start or 00:00-24:00 full-day interval, if either provider emits it, becomes CLOSED before the service can distinguish midnight from a closed marker.
impact: A 24-hour or midnight-opening pharmacy or hospital can be hidden from open-now results or reported closed/unknown when it is open.
preconditions: A government provider uses numeric zero as midnight or full-day schedule semantics rather than the closed marker asserted by target code and tests.
counterevidence: The immutable target's domain comment and tests define 0 and 0000 as closed, and checked-in fixtures contain no zero-valued full-day or midnight schedule.
proof_gaps: No target fixture, contract, or provider sample establishes the candidate's required alternate zero semantics.

evidence.claim: Official weekly fields are copied as strings when both endpoints are present.
HIRA start/end values enter the same interval parser.
Both 0 and 0000 map to null, and either null endpoint collapses the interval to CLOSED.
The collapsed interval becomes UNKNOWN or CLOSED and can be removed from open-now results.
The collapsed interval becomes UNKNOWN or CLOSED and can be removed from open-now results.

affected_locations.label: externally_controlled_source
affected_locations.detail: Official weekly fields are copied as strings when both endpoints are present.

affected_locations.label: externally_controlled_source
affected_locations.detail: HIRA start/end values enter the same interval parser.

affected_locations.label: root_control
affected_locations.detail: Both 0 and 0000 map to null, and either null endpoint collapses the interval to CLOSED.

affected_locations.label: sink_1
affected_locations.detail: The collapsed interval becomes UNKNOWN or CLOSED and can be removed from open-now results.

affected_locations.label: sink_2
affected_locations.detail: The collapsed interval becomes UNKNOWN or CLOSED and can be removed from open-now results.

fix_direction: No security fix is justified from target objects; reopen only with authoritative provider documentation or a captured row showing zero means midnight or full-day.
review_tier: not reportable under the AGENTS security-review boundary
rationale: The candidate's source precondition is not present in target objects and is contradicted by the target's explicit schedule contract and tests. The hypothetical alternate encoding is therefore not reportable.

source: A prompt-injection payload or unfaithful provider response on a phrase not caught by deterministic triage.
closest_control: The server accepts schema-valid ROUTINE or URGENT classifications without a fail-closed semantic check that the non-emergency classification is true.
sink: A false non-emergency urgency value is copied into the accepted answer and the emergency branch is not activated.
impact: A missed emergency can be presented as routine or urgent, delaying emergency care.
preconditions: An emergency phrase misses deterministic triage, the provider returns a schema-valid routine or urgent classification, and no later safety gate detects the false non-emergency state.
counterevidence: Known red flags short-circuit before the model, model-selected emergency must carry the 119 action, and the prompt plus disclaimer remain visible controls.
proof_gaps: A concrete reviewed emergency phrase and controlled provider response must reproduce an accepted false non-emergency result.

evidence.claim: The model-authored urgency classification is copied unchanged into the grounded answer.
The validator constrains an emergency selected by the model but cannot reject a false non-emergency selection.

affected_locations.label: classification_acceptance
affected_locations.detail: The model-authored urgency classification is copied unchanged into the grounded answer.

affected_locations.label: one_way_validator
affected_locations.detail: The validator constrains an emergency selected by the model but cannot reject a false non-emergency selection.

fix_direction: Expand clinically reviewed deterministic triage and server-own safety classifications for supported categories rather than trusting model non-emergency semantics.
review_tier: conditional P0: required clinical, provider, deployment, or runtime proof is unresolved
rationale: The one-way validator cannot reject false routine or urgent output, but the broad missed-emergency claim still depends on a concrete rule miss and provider result. A reproduced miss bypasses §2-4 and is P0; current proof remains conditional.

source: A delegated Bash request invokes git commit -a or git commit --all.
closest_control: The commit policy checks hook-bypass flags but does not classify commit's own broad tracked-file staging mode.
sink: Git stages and commits every modified tracked file rather than only task-scoped files.
impact: Unrelated tracked changes, including weakened safety controls or sensitive configuration, can be swept into a commit.
preconditions: A delegated shell executes git commit -a or git commit --all in a dirty worktree while the hook is expected to enforce scoped changes.
counterevidence: The impact is local to the developer repository and the pre-commit secret scan may catch some recognizable credentials.

evidence.claim: The commit branch checks no-verify but does not reject -a or --all.

affected_locations.label: commit_broad_stage_residue
affected_locations.detail: The commit branch checks no-verify but does not reject -a or --all.

fix_direction: Reject commit broad-stage flags and require explicit, reviewed path staging before commit.
review_tier: P2: validated local developer-integrity gap below the repository's P0/P1 care-impact budget
rationale: Git commit -a/--all deterministically sweeps tracked work into a local commit, but pre-commit and review controls remain, no --no-verify path is claimed, and no direct care or §2 effect is shown. This matches the P2 defense-in-depth class of R01-CAN-006.

source: A Bash request using Git's modern or equivalent destructive operations, such as git restore --worktree . or git clean -fdx.
closest_control: The policy denies selected command names rather than enforcing allowed, path-scoped repository mutations.
sink: Git overwrites tracked worktree content or permanently deletes untracked/ignored files after the hook returns no reason.
impact: Unrelated developer work, local configuration, or untracked evidence can be destroyed despite the policy's stated protection against destructive repository operations.
preconditions: A delegated shell executes git restore, git clean, or an equivalent destructive subcommand while modified, untracked, or ignored files exist.
counterevidence: The boundary is a local agent with the user's shell authority rather than a remote web attacker.

evidence.claim: The Git dispatch handles reset and one checkout form but has no branch for restore, clean, or other worktree-destructive operations.

affected_locations.label: root_control
affected_locations.detail: The Git dispatch handles reset and one checkout form but has no branch for restore, clean, or other worktree-destructive operations.

fix_direction: Do not issue separately; absorb the restore and clean evidence into R01-CAN-005 and apply that canonical finding's complete remediation.
review_tier: not a separate finding: remediation-subsumed duplicate of R01-CAN-005
rationale: R01-CAN-005 already contains a complete-tuple Round-03 recurrence for equivalent destructive Git operations, including pinned probes where git restore . and git clean -fd return no denial. This row has the same source, control, sink, impact, and remediation, so a second finding would double-count it.

source: A command references a secret-bearing environment-file variant such as .env.local or another .env.* path.
closest_control: Protected-path classification recognizes only a path exactly named .env or ending in /.env.
sink: A reader that is otherwise permitted can print the variant file into model-visible tool output and logs.
impact: Server keys or credentials stored in environment-file variants can cross the model and logging boundary.
preconditions: A secret-bearing .env.* variant exists and a delegated reader or source command prints or loads it through the hook.
counterevidence: Literal .env is blocked, ordinary web users have no shell path, and exploitation requires the variant file to exist at the developer boundary.

evidence.claim: Only literal .env path forms are classified as sensitive; .env.* variants remain outside the protected path class.

affected_locations.label: environment_variant_path_classifier
affected_locations.detail: Only literal .env path forms are classified as sensitive; .env.* variants remain outside the protected path class.

fix_direction: Classify .env and every secret-bearing .env.* basename as protected, explicitly exempt only reviewed example or template files, and apply the rule to all readers and source commands.
review_tier: P1 blocker: validated care, safety, privacy, availability, or control impact under the AGENTS review lens
rationale: is_real_env recognizes only the literal basename, so common secret-bearing variants bypass every reader and source guard. This can disclose developer credentials to model-visible output and logs, a validated P1 local secret-boundary failure.

source: An attacker or adjacent process with write access to the configured Redis can replace a cache value and choose Jackson type metadata within an allowed package prefix.
closest_control: BasicPolymorphicTypeValidator blocks types outside the four prefixes, cache values are normally server-authored, and no public HTTP route writes arbitrary Redis bytes. A concrete allowed gadget and production Redis reachability are both unproven.
sink: GenericJackson2JsonRedisSerializer invokes a DefaultTyping.EVERYTHING mapper whose validator allows all com.mermaid, java.util, java.time, and java.lang subtypes rather than the finite cache DTO set.
impact: A malicious cache entry may construct unexpected application/JDK object graphs, causing deserialization denial of service or, if an allowed gadget exists in the resolved classpath, stronger code-execution effects.
preconditions: An attacker or adjacent process can write the correct Redis cache key and value, choose allowed-package type metadata, and an allowed gadget or structure produces meaningful deserialization impact.
counterevidence: Cache values are normally server-authored, the type validator blocks packages outside four prefixes, no public HTTP route writes arbitrary Redis bytes, and Redis write access already permits direct cache corruption or denial of service.
proof_gaps: Production Redis reachability and authentication are unknown, and no concrete allowed gadget or cache-value payload is established in the target classpath.

evidence.claim: DefaultTyping.EVERYTHING accepts subtype metadata for every class under four broad package prefixes.
GenericJackson2JsonRedisSerializer reads cache-controlled JSON using the polymorphic mapper.
The repository's development stack publishes unauthenticated Redis on the host; production topology is not specified.

affected_locations.label: root_control
affected_locations.detail: DefaultTyping.EVERYTHING accepts subtype metadata for every class under four broad package prefixes.

affected_locations.label: deserialization_sink
affected_locations.detail: GenericJackson2JsonRedisSerializer reads cache-controlled JSON using the polymorphic mapper.

affected_locations.label: adjacent_exposure
affected_locations.detail: The repository's development stack publishes unauthenticated Redis on the host; production topology is not specified.

fix_direction: Do not issue separately; preserve the broad-type and missing-gadget notes on R01-CAN-131 and apply that finding's Redis isolation, authentication, and strict cache-typing remediation.
review_tier: not a separate finding: remediation-subsumed duplicate of R01-CAN-131
rationale: R01-CAN-131 already has the same Redis-write precondition and polymorphic serializer control, reports cache denial and serializer-compatible poisoning, records that no allowed gadget is shown, and requires strict cache typing. No concrete gadget or independent stronger sink survives as a separate finding.

source: A structurally valid DUR warning row omits ITEM_SEQ.
closest_control: The row parser silently continues instead of marking the warning result incomplete or invalid.
sink: The identityless warning disappears and the resulting warning list is attached as an ordinary result.
impact: A real contraindication can be omitted while downstream copy states that no DUR warning exists.
preconditions: A successful DUR response contains a real warning row without ITEM_SEQ and product detail assembly consumes the resulting list.
counterevidence: Correct provider rows normally include ITEM_SEQ, no checked-in fixture demonstrates the malformed case, and some other DUR fields are legitimately absent.
proof_gaps: No captured live row establishes current occurrence; reportability rests on the external-boundary fail-open behavior.

evidence.claim: A DUR row without ITEM_SEQ is silently skipped.
The incomplete warning list is attached without an unknown or incomplete state.
An empty list is described as no published contraindication.

affected_locations.label: identityless_row_drop
affected_locations.detail: A DUR row without ITEM_SEQ is silently skipped.

affected_locations.label: ordinary_result_sink
affected_locations.detail: The incomplete warning list is attached without an unknown or incomplete state.

affected_locations.label: no_warning_copy_sink
affected_locations.detail: An empty list is described as no published contraindication.

fix_direction: Treat an identityless DUR row as incomplete or source-unavailable and never translate its silent drop into a server-authored no-warning statement.
review_tier: P1 blocker: validated care, safety, privacy, availability, or control impact under the AGENTS review lens
rationale: The parser silently drops the row and downstream code treats the incomplete list as ordinary, including copy that says no contraindication was published. That can hide safety data and is P1 under the care-information lens.

source: Any caller repeats a cached search/detail key before the six-hour TTL expires.
closest_control: TTL limits staleness, but cached values do not carry their actual fetch time and SourceRef is created outside the cached client method.
sink: The server publishes request-time as retrievedAt for older cached medical data.
impact: Licence, ingredient or warning data up to six hours old appears freshly retrieved, weakening a server-owned provenance signal.
preconditions: A cache hit occurs within the six-hour TTL and DrugService assembles a response by calling source() after reading cached facts.
counterevidence: The TTL is only six hours, public data changes slowly, and the underlying facts may remain accurate during that window.

evidence.claim: External DTOs are cached without retrieval time.
Warnings are cached without retrieval time.
Entries can be six hours old.
Instant.now(clock) is generated on every assembly, including cache hits.
The system context says the records were retrieved just now.

affected_locations.label: source
affected_locations.detail: External DTOs are cached without retrieval time.

affected_locations.label: source
affected_locations.detail: Warnings are cached without retrieval time.

affected_locations.label: root_control
affected_locations.detail: Entries can be six hours old.

affected_locations.label: sink
affected_locations.detail: Instant.now(clock) is generated on every assembly, including cache hits.

affected_locations.label: sink
affected_locations.detail: The system context says the records were retrieved just now.

fix_direction: Cache retrieval time and origin with each provider DTO or SourceRef and preserve that original timestamp on every cache hit.
review_tier: P0 blocker: validated AGENTS section 2 invariant bypass
rationale: Cached facts do not carry fetch time, while source() stamps Instant.now on every assembly and the prompt says retrieved just now. This deterministically falsifies server-owned provenance and directly bypasses AGENTS §2-9, so it is P0.

source: A delegated Bash request uses an alternate destructive filesystem utility such as find -delete.
closest_control: The repository policy enumerates rm forms and does not classify equivalent destructive filesystem operations by effect.
sink: The utility deletes task-external files after the hook returns no denial reason.
impact: Unrelated work, local configuration, or scan evidence can be destroyed despite the repository mutation boundary.
preconditions: A delegated shell uses find -delete or an equivalent destructive filesystem utility while task-external files are reachable.
counterevidence: The boundary is a local agent with shell authority, and the hook does block selected rm -rf forms.

evidence.claim: The deny logic enumerates selected rm and Git forms but not equivalent destructive filesystem utilities.

affected_locations.label: rm_only_effect_policy
affected_locations.detail: The deny logic enumerates selected rm and Git forms but not equivalent destructive filesystem utilities.

fix_direction: Replace command-name enumeration with effect- and path-scoped mutation controls and require explicit approval for destructive filesystem operations.
review_tier: P2: validated local developer-integrity gap below the repository's P0/P1 care-impact budget
rationale: Find -delete is a distinct effect-based policy gap, but the demonstrated effect is privileged local deletion with no direct care path or §2 invariant bypass. That is the same P2 local-authority class as the recursive-deletion finding R01-CAN-004.

source: Anonymous consultation text contains a denial, resolved history, quotation, hypothetical, or other non-current mention of a lexical red flag.
closest_control: EmergencyTriage applies context-free matcher.find() patterns to every concatenated user turn.
sink: Any match returns the server-authored 119 response before retrieval or model processing; retained history can repeat the short circuit on later turns.
impact: Ordinary care information can remain unavailable and the user can be repeatedly directed to unnecessary emergency-service use.
preconditions: A message contains a negated, historical, quoted, hypothetical, or resolved lexical red flag and retained user turns replay that text on later requests.
counterevidence: The source explicitly prefers false positives because false negatives may be much more harmful, and over-eager escalation may be intentional safety policy.
proof_gaps: Qualified clinical and product reviewers must approve the context truth table and distinguish acceptable conservative escalation from harmful over-triage.

evidence.claim: documented tradeoff and root control
all-turn context collapse
emergency-only short circuit
retained user-turn replay

fix_direction: Use a clinically reviewed, server-owned context decision that preserves affirmative and mixed-clause escalation; do not apply a generic nearby-not rule or delegate triage to the model.
review_tier: conditional P1: required clinical, provider, deployment, or runtime proof is unresolved
rationale: Context-free matching and repeated emergency-only short circuit are deterministic. Whether the resulting false positives are reportable harm rather than the documented conservative policy is a clinical/product decision, so P1 remains conditional.

source: Government ingredient text or user input contains a token in the in-code FORM_QUALIFIERS set.
closest_control: Qualifier stripping runs outside the human-signed synonym metadata before identity classification.
sink: The collapsed value compares as EXACT and AllergyChecker can author BLOCKED.
impact: A clinically invalid equivalence can create a false high-authority allergy block; an incorrect omission can leave a real equivalence unmatched.
preconditions: A current FORM_QUALIFIERS token is not identity-preserving in the provider-side or user-side context, yet stripping makes the values compare as exact.
counterevidence: The code gives concrete false-negative reasons for physical-form stripping, deliberately excludes salts, and behavior proof does not show that any current qualifier is clinically wrong.
proof_gaps: A qualified human reviewer must assess every qualifier for both provider-side and user-side contexts and pin reviewed versus unreviewed verdicts.

evidence.claim: unreviewed qualifier authority
collapse and exact identity
server-authored block sink

fix_direction: Move qualifier equivalence into human-reviewed, source-backed metadata or retain transformation provenance so an unreviewed qualifier can yield only warning or unknown, never exact blocking authority.
review_tier: conditional P0: required clinical, provider, deployment, or runtime proof is unresolved
rationale: The in-code list deterministically creates exact and server-authored blocked states outside the reviewed synonym metadata. P0 allergy impact exists only if a current qualifier is clinically invalid, which central validation cannot decide without qualified review.

source: Anonymous chat sends malformed JSON whose unquoted token contains symptom or identifying text.
closest_control: HttpMessageNotReadableException is absent from the sanitized invalid-request handler.
sink: The outer catch-all logs the full Spring/Jackson exception and cause, including the attacker token.
impact: Health-text fragments can persist in application, container, or aggregated logs and widen access to consultation data.
preconditions: An anonymous client sends malformed JSON with an unquoted token containing health or identifying text, target Spring and Jackson parse it, and server error logging is retained or observed.
counterevidence: The client receives a generic INTERNAL_ERROR response, parser token length is bounded, and disclosure is limited to server-side logs rather than the HTTP body.

evidence.claim: raw JSON boundary
missing malformed-body classification
full Throwable log sink
SHA-256 3272169c3d67594d380c2720214ea3d10095c5c14eac2e88a4db5544913b77f1; clean-server response is HTTP 500 with request ID fc4b4a52-d125-4c3d-8c09-8df64fe74ae2
SHA-256 d50e3107bf6263d59bafe5ecc4527e04c6cdd9bb4181d06e39ca86f7e6f1a8c0; client-visible response is generic and carries the same request ID

SHA-256 0fdc2a3191f982be4877a768d223907e4c4fb58271d70924df0bf3bd0a744f9a; 67-line server log quotes synthetic health token chest_pain_and_cannot_breathe_R06CAN003 in both HttpMessageNotReadableException and JsonParseException
Scan-root SHA-256 manifest binds the three sealed dynamic artifacts; sha256sum -c passes.

fix_direction: Handle HttpMessageNotReadableException in the sanitized invalid-request branch and log only fixed, value-free metadata without Throwable messages or causes.
review_tier: P0 blocker: validated AGENTS section 2 invariant bypass
rationale: A clean-server HTTP reproduction at execution revision f68cc39948bdb11139af07017336e45ef07e2325, whose relevant target files have an empty diff from the immutable target, returned a generic HTTP 500 with request ID fc4b4a52-d125-4c3d-8c09-8df64fe74ae2 while the corresponding full-Throwable server log persisted synthetic health token chest_pain_and_cannot_breathe_R06CAN003 in both HttpMessageNotReadableException and JsonParseException. This directly reproduces the §2-5 bypass and validates P0.
